analysisSummary: Confirmed by Skeptic: VITE_DEV_LOGIN_ENABLED alone cannot bypass in production. Vite's import.meta.env.DEV is false in production builds regardless of VITE_DEV_LOGIN_ENABLED. The security boundary is enforced by Vite's own build system. False positive.
Dev mode is intentionally permissive. Production enforces signed sessions. Server-side verification is the security boundary, not client-side. Not a bug.

Confirmed by Skeptic: Both VITE_DEV_OTP_BYPASS and hostname check must be true. Vite's DEV flag and hostname check together prevent production exploitation. The dual-gate design is sound. False positive.

Confirmed: first sync failure causes empty cache. Fixed admin still works (from env var). Impact is that additional admin users cannot login after sync failure. Real bug but low severity — graceful degradation means app is not fully broken.
suggestedFix: Keep existing cache on sync failure: catch (e) { console.error(...); if (_usersCache === null) _usersCache = []; } — only sets empty if no cache exists. Current code already does this (line 165). The actual issue is lack of retry. Consider adding retry mechanism.

analysisSummary: Skeptic is correct. Server-side rate limit is authoritative. Client-side rate limit is UX only. The real defense (3 OTP per NIK per 15 min via RPC) cannot be bypassed client-side. Not a bug.
